region_of_origin: Vietnam (French-colonial origin); widely available at Vietnamese bakeries and restaurants in Singapore
food_subgroup: Vietnamese rice-wheat hybrid baguette filled with pork liver pâté, char siu pork, pickled carrot and daikon, cucumber, coriander, and Maggi seasoning

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific entry for Vietnamese Bánh Mì. Closest defensible parent is FOODON_03311832 sandwich (food). A more specific FoodOn term does not exist as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Sandwich — Vietnamese baguette (wheat/rice flour hybrid) filled with pork liver pâté, char siu pork, pickled vegetables, and Vietnamese condiments
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CAJ is the closest defensible parent for a filled baguette-style sandwich. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Bánh Mì as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Bánh Mì or Vietnamese baguette sandwich as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Bánh Mì baguette sandwich weighs approximately 220 g and constitutes a complete single-meal serving.

nutrient_source.title: FAO/INFOODS Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia — Sandwich / Mixed Pork Dish composite; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for French bread, pork liver pâté, char siu pork, and pickled vegetables
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia; USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a complete Bánh Mì sandwich. Values are derived from FAO/INFOODS SEA composite data for Vietnamese pork baguette sandwich, cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries (French bread USDA #18033, pork liver pâté USDA #07058, char siu pork, pickled daikon/carrot). The composite per-100g values are consistent with the input record and are accepted as adapted estimates. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and water content are not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA composite and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values — French baguette (wheat flour) GI ~70–75; Vietnamese baguette adjusted downward to ~68 due to rice flour addition reducing starch gelatinisation rate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese Bánh Mì baguette. The University of Sydney GI Database records French baguette (white wheat flour) at GI 70–75. The Vietnamese baguette uses a wheat-rice flour hybrid producing a lighter, more airy crumb; rice flour addition is documented to reduce starch gelatinisation and lower GI by approximately 3–7 units relative to pure wheat baguette. GI 68 is a defensible analogous estimate. GL per 220g serving = (68 × 26.5 × 2.20) / 100 = 39.6, rounded to 39.5 as reported in source record.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (645 mg/100g; ~1,419 mg per 220g sandwich) from pork liver pâté, char siu, cured ham, and Maggi seasoning sauce
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit Bánh Mì to no more than once per week, request reduced Maggi sauce and less pâté, and be aware that a single sandwich delivers approximately 59% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,400 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~39.5) driven by 58.3g total carbohydrate in a moderate-high GI refined wheat-rice baguette; equivalent carbohydrate load to approximately 2 cups of steamed white rice
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should consume only half a Bánh Mì per sitting, prioritise eating the protein filling (pâté, char siu) before the bread to blunt glycaemic response, avoid sweet sauces, and monitor post-meal blood glucose carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (645 mg/100g), moderate saturated fat (3.2 g/100g), and cholesterol (75 mg/100g) from pork liver pâté and char siu; partially offset by low trans fat (0.1 g/100g) and beneficial micronutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit Bánh Mì frequency due to its high sodium and moderate saturated fat load, request reduced pâté and no Maggi sauce to lower the sodium-saturated fat burden, and pair with a low-sodium meal for the remainder of the day.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beneficial micronutrients from pork liver pâté including vitamin A (85 mcg RAE/100g), vitamin B12 (2.2 mcg/100g), and iron (2.5 mg/100g) support anti-inflammatory pathways and address RA-associated anaemia
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Bánh Mì is generally suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the pork liver pâté provides clinically useful vitamin A, B12, and iron, though the high-GI baguette may contribute to proinflammatory insulin spikes and should be moderated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh Mì is one of the most celebrated examples of culinary fusion in Southeast Asia — a Vietnamese sandwich born from French colonial influence that transformed the European baguette into something distinctly Vietnamese. The bread itself is a hybrid: wheat flour blended with rice flour to produce a lighter, crispier, thinner-crusted roll with a more airy, open crumb than a traditional French baguette, and a GI slightly lower (~68) than pure wheat baguette due to the rice flour component. Split lengthwise, the roll is spread generously with savoury pork liver pâté, layered with slices of char siu pork (Chinese-influenced sweet-roasted pork) and Vietnamese ham or head cheese, then filled with pickled carrot and daikon (đồ chua — the essential sweet-sour crunch), thin cucumber slices, fresh coriander, spring onion, and a drizzle of Maggi seasoning sauce (the MSG-rich, umami backbone). Optional additions include chilli slices or Sriracha. The result is a complex interplay of textures — crisp crust, chewy crumb, tender meat, crunchy pickles — and flavours — savoury, sweet, sour, spicy, and herbal — in a compact, portable package. A standard 220g sandwich provides approximately 539 kcal, 27.5g protein, 21.6g fat, and 58.3g carbohydrate. Sodium is very high at approximately 1,419 mg per sandwich, primarily from Maggi seasoning, pork liver pâté, and cured char siu. The pork liver pâté contributes clinically significant vitamin A (85 mcg RAE/100g), vitamin B12 (2.2 mcg/100g), and iron (2.5 mg/100g). In Singapore, Bánh Mì is available at Vietnamese bakeries and restaurants and is popular as a grab-and-go breakfast or lunch. It is not suitable for patients with hypertension or type 2 diabetes without significant modification.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a complete Bánh Mì sandwich. Values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA composite data cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries. The composite is internally consistent and nutritionally plausible. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using French baguette as the reference food with a documented downward adjustment for rice flour content. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted composite methodology.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for the Vietnamese pork baguette sandwich composite are not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the assembled composite sandwich is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA table. All other micronutrient values are supported by the FAO/INFOODS SEA composite and USDA SR Legacy cross-reference. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate based on French baguette GI 70–75 (University of Sydney GI Database) adjusted downward for rice flour content in the Vietnamese hybrid baguette. GL per 220g serving calculated as (68 × 26.5 × 2.20) / 100 = 39.6, consistent with the input record value of 39.5. The food is not halal-certified due to pork content. Hindi local name has been added to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Singaporean dishes, as Banh Mi is widely available in Singapore.
